title: Affine interpolation preserves constant data

source.citation: Alfio Quarteroni, Riccardo Sacco, and Fausto Saleri, Numerical Mathematics, 2nd ed., Springer, 2007, Ch. 8 (Polynomial Interpolation).
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: Affine interpolation between two equal endpoint values returns that constant for every parameter.
assumptions: theta is a real interpolation parameter
informal.expected_conclusion: The affine interpolant of constant data is constant.

declarations.name: affineInterp
declarations.statement: def affineInterp (theta y0 y1 : Real) : Real := (1 - theta) * y0 + theta * y1

declarations.name: affineInterp_const
declarations.statement: theorem affineInterp_const (theta c : Real) : affineInterp theta c c = c

tasks.input: Translate the interpolation theorem `affineInterp_const` into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem affineInterp_const (theta c : Real) : affineInterp theta c c = c

tasks.input: Identify the main algebraic or order lemma needed for the interpolation proof.
